explanations.6: The port 6379 is exposed without mapping to a host port—containers can reach it, but the outside world cannot unless you specify a host port like '6379:6379'.
explanations.10: This means Docker will run 2 copies of the Redis service across your cluster for load balancing and redundancy.
explanations.12: Parallelism means how many replicas get updated at the same time; setting it to 2 means both Redis copies update simultaneously rather than one at a time.
explanations.25: This constraint forces the database to only run on manager nodes in your Docker Swarm cluster, ensuring it stays in a controlled location.
explanations.32: The vote service won't start until Redis is ready, preventing errors from connection attempts to a service that doesn't exist yet.
explanations.59: The worker service connects to both frontend and backend networks so it can talk to both the voting app and the database.
explanations.62: Mode 'replicated' means you want multiple copies running (as opposed to 'global' which runs one on every node).
explanations.64: Labels are metadata tags you can attach to containers to identify or organize them; this one marks the service as part of the VOTING app.
explanations.67: Delay is the waiting period before Docker attempts to restart a failed container; 10 seconds gives the service time to cool down.
explanations.68: Max attempts limits restart tries to 3; if it fails 3 times, Docker stops trying to restart it automatically.
explanations.69: Window is a time span (120 seconds here) during which Docker counts restart attempts; failures older than 2 minutes don't count toward the max.
explanations.77: Stop grace period gives the visualizer 90 seconds to shut down gracefully before Docker force-kills it.
explanations.79: This mounts the Docker daemon's socket file into the container so the visualizer can ask Docker for information about running services.
